scene: A stick-figure man at a jewelry store counter, wide-eyed, holding up a single sparkling diamond ring, his open wallet emptying beside him on the counter. Selective pop color: blue-white diamond sparkle. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.

The same stick-figure man with a happy thought-bubble above his head showing himself down on one knee proposing, certain and content, believing it is how it has always been. Selective pop color: the blue-white diamond. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.

The man's happy thought-bubble popping and dissolving into little fragments, his certain face faltering and confused, the so-called tradition was never real. Stark black ink on plain white. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.

A vintage old-fashioned couple from a century ago getting engaged with no ring at all, simply holding hands and smiling, plainly dressed in old-timey clothes. No ring and no diamond anywhere in frame. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.

A mysterious shadowy black silhouette hand reaching in from off the edge of the frame into the back of the modern stick-figure man's head, planting a glowing ring-shaped idea into his mind, the source staying hidden and mysterious. Selective pop color: the glowing blue-white ring idea. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.

The stick-figure man walking away beaming and clutching the diamond ring, totally oblivious, with faint puppet strings trailing up from his arms to an unseen hand somewhere above the frame. Oblivious and happy. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.

A wall mirror in which the man's reflection has been replaced by a plain blank stick figure representing the viewer, also holding the same sparkling diamond ring and looking back out of the mirror, the trick now turned on you. Selective pop color: the blue-white diamond in the reflection. No text, no letters, no numbers, no symbols.
